THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT FILE AN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION, AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT TIMELY CONTEST THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. PARTIES WILL MEET AND CONFER WITH REGARD TO THE FURTHER RESPONSES ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 23, 2015 AT PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARILYN BUNANG, DALILA AHUMADA AND PATRICIA MELGAR'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF JANET TAPIA'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF GOVERNMENT CODE 11135 FAILS AS TO THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS BECAUSE THAT CODE SECTION ONLY APPLIES TO A "LOCAL AGENCY, OR PERSON, WHO REGULARLY EMPLOYS FIVE OR MORE PERSONS AND WHO RECEIVES STATE SUPPORT" IN EXCESS OF CERTAIN AMOUNTS. (22 CCR, 98010; AIKINS V. ST. HELENA HOSPITAL (ND CA 1994) 843 F. SUPP. 1329, 1340-41.) MS. TAPIA'S SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION IS NOT TIME-BARRED AS A MATTER OF LAW. WHILE GATTO V. COUNTY OF SONOMA (2002) 98 CAL.APP.4TH 744, 759 HELD THAT A ONE YEAR LIMITATIONS PERIOD APPLIES TO UNRUH ACT CLAIMS, IT DID SO BECAUSE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 340 THEN PROVIDED FOR A ONE YEAR LIMITATIONS PERIOD FOR ACTIONS SEEKING DAMAGES FOR INJURY CAUSED BY THE WRONGFUL ACT OR NEGLECT OF ANOTHER. HOWEVER, CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 340 NO LONGER APPLIES TO SUCH A CLAIM AND THE CURRENT STATUTE THAT DOES, CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 335.1, PROVIDES FOR A TWO YEAR LIMITATIONS PERIOD. SINCE THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED AT LEAST IN PART ON ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT THAT OCCURRED ON JUNE 19, 2014, THAT CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT TIME BARRED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS ARRIBA JUNTOS AND DALILA AHUMADA'S Motion For Terminating, Issue, Evidentiary, And/Or Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Due To Plaintiffs Willful Failure To Comply With Courts January 12 2016 Order Regarding Her Delivery Of Records From Westside Community Integrated Services To Defendants Counsel is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff Janet Tapia is ordered to fully comply within 10 days with the Court's January 8, 2016 order regarding documents subpoenaed from Westside Community Integrated Services. If plaintiff has not fully complied within the 10 days, evidentiary sanctions will be imposed to prohibit her from introducing the following: (a) any of her records from Westside, (b) any testimony from Westside health-care providers, (c) any testimony regarding past or future treatment from Westside and (d) evidence regarding any past or present medications prescribed by Westside. Plaintiff shall pay $2,576 in sanctions to defendants' counsel within 30 days of service of this order for the costs of having to bring this motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Janet Tapia telephone number 510-823-5373 in Propria Persona.
Peter Finn of Bradley Curley Asiano Barrabee Abel & Lowalski telephone number 415-464-8888 for Defendants Arriba Juntos, Dalila Ahumada, Marilyn Bunag and Patricia Melgar.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MARILYN BUNANG, DALILA AHUMADA AND PATRICIA MELGAR'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action and overruled as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff Janet Tapia's first cause of action for violation of Government Code 11135 fails as to the individual defendants because that code section only applies to a "local agency, or person, who regularly employs five or more persons and who receives state support" in excess of certain amounts. (22 CCR, 98010; Aikins v. St. Helena Hospital (ND CA 1994) 843 F. Supp. 1329, 1340-41.) Ms. Tapia's second cause of action for violation is not time-barred as a matter of law. While Gatto v. County of Sonoma (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 744, 759 held that a one year limitations period applies to Unruh Act claims, it did so because Code of Civil Procedure 340 then provided for a one year limitations period for actions seeking damages for injury caused by the wrongful act or neglect of another. However, Code of Civil Procedure 340 no longer applies to such a claim and the current statute that does, Code of Civil Procedure 335.1, provides for a two year limitations period. Since the second cause of action is based at least in part on alleged wrongful conduct that occurred on June 19, 2014, that cause of action is not time barred. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
